title: Power Isn't Just a Utility Anymore -It's a Business Risk
snippet:
Power isn’t just a utility anymore—it’s becoming a liability.  
  
Between rate hikes and outages, it feels like businesses are stuck paying more for something they have less control over.  
  
I’ve been looking into alternatives and came across what dGen Energy Partners is doing with Mainspring Linear Generators.  
  
From what I’m seeing, it’s basically on-site power generation at a more predictable cost (they’re quoting roughly 9–16¢/kWh), with the ability to stay connected to the grid or ope
ai_summary: General discussion of business energy cost management and alternatives, reflecting market pain points that drive demand for renewable suppliers like tem. No specific competitor or regulatory trigger.
ai_reasoning: This is background industry sentiment about businesses seeking alternatives to traditional utility power due to cost and reliability concerns. It touches on tem's addressable market (I&C customers seeking control over energy), but is generic Reddit discussion without specific regulatory, competitive, or brand relevance. Score 5 as worth understanding market attitudes, but no immediate action needed.
